name: Be the Beat
short_description: An AI-powered boombox that turns a dancer's freestyle movements into matching music recommendations, reversing the usual relationship where music leads dance.
long_description: Be the Beat is an interactive boombox that converts physical body movement into sound. It uses PoseNet to capture a dancer's live movements, an LLM to interpret their style, energy, and tempo, and the Spotify API to surface songs that match, all triggered by a hand clap and returned within about five seconds. By treating an open-ended range of gestures as input rather than a fixed gesture vocabulary, it reframes the dancer as the source of the music and the device as a responsive, expressive partner.

images.caption: Be the Beat, an AI boombox that generates music from a dancer's freestyle movement.
images.alt_text: A stylized boombox device presented as the hero image for the Be the Beat project, which converts dance movement into music.

sources.title: Be the Beat
sources.access_notes: Creator's own project page; primary source.

interactions.title: Clap, freestyle, and let the room pick the song
interactions.description: Clap once to start recording, throw a short freestyle, and within about five seconds the boombox reads your movement and starts playing a Spotify track that matches its style, energy, and tempo.

interactions.title: Re-clap to push the set in a new direction
interactions.description: Unhappy with the pick or feeling a different mood, clap again to re-run the analysis; the recommendations refresh as your freestyle develops, turning the device into a back-and-forth partner rather than a one-shot jukebox.

interactions.title: The machine can tell house from hip-hop
interactions.description: In pilot trials the boombox matched music to the tempo of a dancer's movements and distinguished the intricacies between house and hip-hop moves, picking up genre-specific footwork rather than just gross motion.

interactions.title: Dancers feel like they are leading, not following
interactions.description: Trial participants reported having more control over artistic expression and described the boombox as a novel way to discover dance genres and choreograph creatively, flipping the usual sense of dancing to a track.

interactions.title: A stage piece where the crowd becomes the DJ
interactions.description: Presented in the NeurIPS Creative AI track as a work that blurs the line between leader and follower; staged at a party it could let whoever steps up and dances steer the playlist for everyone, no phone or DJ booth involved.

interactions.title: Coach a beginner toward a genre by moving like it
interactions.description: Because the LLM reads open-ended movement rather than a fixed gesture list, a learner could try out tentative steps and use the returned tracks as feedback on which genre their body is drifting toward, grounded in the pose-to-style pipeline it already runs.

interactions.title: Warm-up that escalates with your energy
interactions.description: Start slow and the boombox surfaces low-tempo tracks; build intensity and the next clap should pull faster, higher-energy songs, using the energy and tempo features it already extracts to ramp a workout or rehearsal.
